text:
Current camera-based 3D object detection methods heavily rely on the spatial location estimation in Bird’s-Eye-View (BEV) and usually perform unsatisfactory depth estimation. In this work, we propose a high-performance 3D object detector named BEVDepth to enhance the depth estimation while maintaining robust spatial location estimation in BEV. We enforce explicit depth supervision on the predicted spatial location with the assistance of point cloud data. Additionally, we design a camera-aware depth estimation module that incorporates the camera intrinsic and extrinsic parameters to augment the network. To eliminate the ambiguity caused by the linguistic and spatial differences in unprojecting image features into 3D space, we propose a Depth Refinement Module to alleviate such errors.

By integrating the above innovations with high-speed Efficient Voxel Pooling and multi-frame fusion, BEVDepth achieves a state-of-the-art result of 60.9% NDS on the challenging nuScenes benchmark, which is the first camera-only depth estimation model to achieve an NDS above 60% while remaining efficient enough for practical use. Code will be open sourced.

In this work, we propose a high-performance 3D object detector named BEVDepth to enhance the depth estimation while maintaining robust spatial location estimation in BEV. We enforce explicit depth supervision on the predicted spatial location with the assistance of point cloud data. Additionally, we design a camera-aware depth estimation module that incorporates the camera intrinsic and extrinsic parameters to augment the network. To eliminate the ambiguity caused by the linguistic and spatial differences in unprojecting image features into 3D space, we propose a Depth Refinement Module to alleviate such errors.

By integrating the above innovations with high-speed Efficient Voxel Pooling and multi-frame fusion, BEVDepth achieves a state-of-the-art result of 60.9% NDS on the challenging nuScenes benchmark, which is the first camera-only depth estimation model to achieve an NDS above 60% while remaining efficient enough for practical use.
